headline: FIRST-BASELINE assessment for Spain (rotation queue, never previously assessed). Published composite 60.9 (Established band, rank 42). Research finds offsetting positive and negative signals: POSITIVE — (1) Jan 27, 2026 mass regularization of ~500,000 undocumented migrants via decree, explicitly framed as 'human rights-based migration model' (counter-trend to Western anti-immigration policies); (2) UN Human Rights Committee Jul 2025 commended Spain's abortion access reforms. NEGATIVE — (1) Active corruption investigations involving Sanchez aides (Cerdan, Abalos, Garcia) and PM's wife; (2) Apr 28, 2026 housing decree blocked in Parliament leaving 'rent shock' — Spain's #1 voter concern unresolved; (3) Migrant reception center overcrowding cited by UN HRC; (4) coalition fragility approaching end of legislature. Net assessment: composite of 60.0 vs published 60.9 — sub-threshold delta of -0.9 pts. Confirmation, no proposal applied.
key_evidence: Jan 27, 2026: Spain approved decree regularizing ~500,000 undocumented migrants; PM framed as 'migration model based on human rights, integration, and coexistence' (CNN, Euronews, La Moncloa).
Application window April 1 - June 30, 2026 — implementation in-window for next cycle assessment.
Jul 2025: UN Human Rights Committee commended Spain's abortion access reforms; raised concerns about migrant reception center overcrowding (OHCHR).
Apr 28, 2026: Sanchez housing decree (rent control extension) blocked in Parliament after Junts opposition; rent shock for tenants now unresolved.
Apr 29, 2026: businessman testimony in 'Koldo Case' implicated Sanchez personally; PSOE legal action vowed against 'slander'.
Cerdan (former senior aide) in pre-trial detention; Abalos and Garcia in custody over face mask contract bribery; Begona Gomez (PM's wife) faces possible trial.
BTI 2026 democracy assessment: 'fourth Sanchez government' instability; coalition relies on Junts per Catalunya.

evidence.finding: Spain to grant legal status to half a million undocumented migrants under a 'migration model based on human rights' (Sanchez quote).
Human Rights Committee experts commended Spain's abortion access measures but asked about accountability for past rights violations and overcrowding in migrant reception centres.
Rent shock after Sanchez housing defeat — decree blocked in Parliament leaves tenants exposed to rent increases and shorter contracts.
Businessman testimony seeks to implicate PM Sanchez personally in corruption trial; PSOE responds with slander legal action.
Spain documented progress on civil rights but ongoing concerns about migrant treatment, racist offenses (+11% reported), housing crisis as #1 voter concern.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.5 (unchanged). Standard institutional detection capacity; no in-window structural change.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.5 (unchanged). Migration regularization signals genuine perspective-taking on undocumented populations; offset by reception-center overcrowding.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.4 (-0.1). Housing decree blocked is a meaningful AC1 Responsiveness gap on the #1 voter concern; partial offset from migration regularization rollout.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.3 (+0.3). EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable strongly elevated by 500K-person regularization; EQ4 Access Design supported by abortion access reforms; partial offset from reception-center overcrowding.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.4 (-0.1). B3 Scope Clarity weakened by inability to deliver promised housing reforms.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.2 (-0.3). AB1 Harm Acknowledgment and AB3 Transparency weakened by sustained corruption investigations involving aides and PM's wife; PSOE response (slander suits) is defensive rather than acknowledgment-first.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.5 (unchanged). Migration regularization is S1 Root Cause Orientation positive; coalition instability tempers the score.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.4 (-0.1). I1 Consistency Under Pressure: migration regularization bears real political cost (counter-trend, polarizing); offset by I4 Values Alignment concerns from corruption pattern.

band_boundary_note: Calculated composite 60.0 is exactly at the 60-point Established/Functional boundary. Confidence is LOW (first-baseline rotation, 0.9-pt delta). Recommendation: flag-for-review, do not auto-apply. Migration regularization implementation (April-June 2026 application window) should be the primary monitor for next cycle.
negativeSignals: Sustained corruption investigations climbing toward PM; housing decree blocked; coalition fragility; migrant reception center overcrowding; +11% racist offenses (HRW).
positiveSignals: 500K-person migration regularization (counter-trend, human-rights framed); abortion access reforms commended by UN HRC; Constitutional Court functioning; minority coalition functioning under democratic pressure.
sector_context: Spain occupies a notable counter-trend position in Western Europe on migration; rotation cycle reveals BTI/HRW evidence of governance fragility offsetting human-rights leadership in specific domains.
open_actions_resolved: Migration regularization captured as EQU/SYS/INT positive evidence
Corruption pattern captured as ACC negative evidence
open_actions_carried: Re-queue Spain after migration regularization application window closes (June 30 2026)
Monitor for new housing legislation post-Apr 28 defeat
